sec.method: APR constraint from fermentation (acarbose by HPLC)

growth_records.cite: Wang, Yali et al. Reconstruction and in silico analysis of an Actinoplanes sp. SE50/110 genome-scale metabolic model for acarbose production. Frontiers in Microbiology (2015). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2015.00632. [PMC4479805]
growth_records.snippet: Table 5 Comparison between the simulation value and fermentation data ... Constraints (mmol/gDCW/h) MUR 0.17 AUR 0.23 APR 0.0024 ; Growth rate In vivo 0.026 In silico 0.024
growth_records.notes: Actinoplanes = prokaryote (Actinobacteria). Table 5 row 1 of 3 experimental fermentation constraints (maltose-uptake rates fitted from different original maltose concentration data) with matched in-vivo growth rate. In-vivo mu=0.026 h-1 reported; doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.026=26.7 h (computed). MUR=maltose uptake rate, AUR=ammonium uptake rate, APR=acarbose production rate, all mmol/gDCW/h. Rates are model constraints derived from measured fermentation data, hence is_experimental:true. Medium = acarbose synthesis medium (Solution 1 + trace-element Solution 2, based on Wendler et al. 2013). Temp 28C, aerobic shaken. pH of acarbose medium not stated (only the separate minimal functional-test medium was set to pH 7.0).

growth_records.snippet: Table 5 ... MUR 0.26 AUR 0.31 APR 0.0054 ; Growth rate In vivo 0.034 In silico 0.032
growth_records.notes: Table 5 row 2 of 3. In-vivo mu=0.034 h-1 reported; td=ln2/0.034=0.693/0.034=20.4 h (computed). MUR/AUR = maltose/ammonium uptake, APR = acarbose secretion (mmol/gDCW/h), from measured fermentation data used as model constraints. Same acarbose synthesis medium, 28C, aerobic.

growth_records.snippet: Table 5 ... MUR 0.27 AUR 0.35 APR 0.0035 ; Growth rate In vivo 0.038 In silico 0.037
growth_records.notes: Table 5 row 3 of 3. In-vivo mu=0.038 h-1 reported; td=ln2/0.038=0.693/0.038=18.2 h (computed). MUR/AUR = maltose/ammonium uptake, APR = acarbose secretion (mmol/gDCW/h), from measured fermentation data as model constraints. Same acarbose synthesis medium, 28C, aerobic. Note APR does not increase monotonically with MUR across the 3 points (0.0024, 0.0054, 0.0035).

yield_fit.note: qS = (1/Yxs)·µ + m_s  — linear fit of carbon-source uptake vs growth rate
